overview: Higgsfield uses the MCSLA formula: Model · Camera · Subject · Look · Action — in that order. Always specify shot structure upfront: number of shots, duration, aspect ratio. Physics language is the superpower: describe what materials DO, not just that things move. Negative camera constraints are as important as positive ones — explicitly state what the camera DOESN'T do to prevent default cutting behavior. For realism, always add 'no 3D, no cartoon, no VFX'.

mcsla_structure.description: Model → Camera → Subject → Look → Action. Always open with: shot count + duration + aspect ratio + visual style. This is the official Higgsfield formula.
best_terms: Static camera, no cuts, no zoom

motion_physics.description: Describe materials IN motion, not motion abstractly. 'Fabric billowing in gusts, pulling at the hem' beats 'windy'. Physics language is Higgsfield's core differentiator.

negative_camera_constraints.description: Explicitly tell the camera what NOT to do — this prevents Higgsfield's default cutting and zooming behavior. 'No cuts, no zoom' is essential for continuous shots.

realism_enforcement.description: Add realism constraints to prevent plasticky/3D render look.
best_terms: practical VFX feel, minimal CGI look

subject_detail.description: Physical description of the subject — clothing material, body language — all feed the physics simulation.

power_patterns: Single continuous shot, [duration], [aspect ratio]. [Camera type, negative constraints]. [Subject with material details]. [Physics descriptor — fabric/hair/liquid behavior]. [Look: cinematic style]. [Action with motion quality].
[Cinematic style]. [Detailed subject in physical material description]. [MCSLA order: camera · subject · look · action]. [Physics language]. No 3D, no cartoon.
antipatterns: 'Stay still' or 'motionless' — Higgsfield defaults to motion. Use 'static camera, subject at rest, micro-movements only' instead.
Vague physics — 'it's windy' → should be 'gusts pulling at the fabric hem, hair streaming horizontal'.
No negative camera constraints — without 'no cuts, no zoom', Higgsfield will default to dynamic cutting.
Too many characters — 1-2 primary subjects max. Group scenes collapse without Soul ID consistency.
Missing opening structure — always start with: shots, duration, aspect ratio, visual standard.

strong: Single continuous shot, 8 seconds, 16:9. Static camera, no cuts, no zoom. Editorial fashion: a woman in a white silk dress stands at cliff edge. Strong wind — silk billowing and whipping violently, fabric pulling and releasing. Hair streaming horizontal. Camera tilts up slowly. Golden hour backlight. No 3D, no cartoon, no VFX.

Single continuous shot, 8 seconds, 9:16. Handheld, completely unstabilized, no smoothing. Cinematic realism. A chef in a commercial kitchen, intense focus. Steam rising from pans, smoke curling upward. Motion blur on fast movements. 35mm film quality. No 3D, no cartoon.
weak: A woman standing looking cool with wind.
Chef in kitchen cooking with steam.
